mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary policy that reduces borrowing costs and boosts equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain negative in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS declines by >0.25pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3% annualized (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank tightening expectations, reducing equity risk premiums and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns exceed 1.5% in 40%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals strong supply-side dynamics and labor market tightness, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns fall below 0.5% when INDPRO rises >1.5% YoY and UNRATE declines >0.3pp.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) during a monetary tightening cycle compress equity valuations via higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns exceed 1.0% in 50%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS rises >0.5pp over 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions that support corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain negative in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS grows >100k/month.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 5% signals labor market overheating, prompting inflationary pressures that reduce equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns exceed 1.2% in 40%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts (HOUST) and consumer spending (RSAFS) indicates strong demand-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns fall below 0.3% when HOUST rises >2% MoM and RSAFS increases >1.5% MoM.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) during a supply shock reduces corporate output and earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns exceed 0.8% in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO falls below 99.0.
